name: Banu Haqim
bane: Blood Addiction. When a Banu Haqim drinks the blood of another Kindred, they must roll Hunger + Blood Potency (Difficulty 2 + Bane Severity). On a failure, they gain a compulsion to continue drinking until the source is drained or they are pulled away. Each drink of Kindred vitae compounds the craving.
compulsion: Judgment. The vampire is compelled to punish someone nearby who has transgressed against their personal moral code. They must openly reprimand, lecture, or physically punish the offender. Until the transgressor has been chastised, the vampire takes a 2-dice penalty to all rolls not related to delivering judgment.

name: Brujah
bane: Short Fuse. The Brujah's Beast is close to the surface. Subtract Bane Severity dice from any roll to resist Frenzy. Brujah enter frenzy more easily than any other clan — even minor provocations can set them off.
compulsion: Rebellion. The vampire must go against the wishes of whoever holds authority in the scene — Prince, boss, Primogen, or anyone giving orders. They must openly defy, contradict, or undermine the authority figure. Until they do, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls. Simply remaining silent is not enough — they must act against the authority.

name: Gangrel
bane: Animal Features. Whenever a Gangrel frenzies, they gain a permanent bestial feature — a patch of fur, a fang that won't retract, claws, a tail nub, slitted pupils, pointed ears, or similar. Each feature reduces one Social Attribute (except Intimidation) by 1, to a minimum of 0. These features are cumulative and cannot be healed by normal means. Fleshcrafting or similar powers can temporarily hide them.
compulsion: Feral Impulses. The vampire regresses to an animalistic state. They must resolve the current situation through fight or flight — complex plans, negotiation, and technology are beyond them. They must choose: attack the nearest threat or flee the scene entirely. Until they commit to one, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all Mental and Social rolls.

name: Hecata
bane: Painful Kiss. The Hecata's bite causes excruciating agony instead of ecstasy. Mortals bitten must succeed on a Stamina + Resolve roll (Difficulty = 2 + Bane Severity) or scream and struggle. The Hecata must grapple or restrain victims to feed, making hunting conspicuous and difficult. Willing vessels still experience pain. The Lingering Kiss Presence power does not mitigate this.
compulsion: Morbidity. The vampire becomes obsessed with death and the dead. They must interact with a corpse, ghost, or memento mori — studying remains, communing with spirits, visiting a grave, or discussing death at length. Until they engage meaningfully with mortality, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls not related to death or the dead.

name: Lasombra
bane: Distorted Reflection. The Lasombra's image is distorted in reflections and recordings. Mirrors show a warped, monstrous visage; photographs blur; video footage glitches and distorts. This makes it impossible to use technology that relies on facial recognition, video calls appear corrupted, and security camera footage of them is obviously supernatural. Subtract Bane Severity from any Technology rolls involving their own image.
compulsion: Ruthlessness. The vampire must prove their superiority by any means necessary. They cannot accept a compromise, show mercy, or take the gracious option. They must win — and be seen to win. Surrender, retreat, and charity are impossible. Until they have demonstrably triumphed over someone in the scene, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls.

name: Malkavian
bane: Fractured Perspective. Each Malkavian suffers from at least one mental disorder that cannot be cured or treated — it is a fundamental part of their vampiric nature. This is selected at character creation (paranoia, dissociation, synesthesia, compulsive behavior, hallucinations, etc.). The condition worsens during stress: in high-pressure situations, suffer a Bane Severity penalty to one category of dice pools (specified at character creation). The condition is always present; it simply becomes debilitating under pressure.
compulsion: Delusion. The vampire's grip on reality fractures. They experience a vivid hallucination, intrusive thought, or false belief that feels utterly real. The specific delusion is tied to their Bane disorder. They must act on the delusion as if it were real — warning others of invisible threats, responding to voices, or insisting on a falsehood. Until the delusion passes (usually one scene), they suffer a 2-dice penalty to Perception and related rolls.

name: Ministry
bane: Vulnerability to Light. The Ministry are hypersensitive to light. They take Bane Severity as additional Aggravated damage from sunlight exposure. Bright artificial light (spotlights, high-beam headlights, flashbangs) causes them to suffer a Bane Severity penalty to all dice pools while exposed. Even normal indoor lighting can be uncomfortable, though not penalizing.
compulsion: Transgression. The vampire is compelled to tempt someone into breaking a moral, ethical, or social rule. They must identify a prohibition the target holds and entice them to violate it — a vow, a law, a taboo, a diet, sobriety, fidelity. The temptation must be genuine and the target must have real choice. Until someone transgresses due to the vampire's influence, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls.

name: Nosferatu
bane: Repulsive. Every Nosferatu is physically monstrous — hideously deformed in a way unique to them. This cannot be hidden by mundane means (makeup, surgery, masks). Their Appearance-related dice pools automatically fail in any situation where their true form is visible. They must rely on Obfuscate (Mask of a Thousand Faces) to pass among mortals. Even Masked, close physical contact (touching, intimate situations) may reveal something wrong. Add Bane Severity to the Difficulty of any Social roll when their true appearance is visible.
compulsion: Cryptophilia. The vampire becomes obsessed with learning a secret. They must uncover hidden information — eavesdrop, hack, investigate, interrogate, or spy. The secret must be genuinely unknown to them and non-trivial. Until they learn something they didn't know before (and that the holder would prefer to keep hidden), they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls not related to information gathering.

name: Ravnos
bane: Doomed. The sun's curse burns the Ravnos more fiercely than other Kindred. When exposed to sunlight, they take additional Aggravated damage equal to their Bane Severity. Additionally, when they would enter torpor, they must make a Humanity roll (Difficulty 2 + Bane Severity) — failure means Final Death as their body crumbles to ash. The Ravnos exist on borrowed time.
compulsion: Tempting Fate. The vampire must take a dramatic, unnecessary risk. They must choose the most dangerous option available — pick the fight they might lose, take the shortcut through hostile territory, make the bet they can't afford. Playing it safe is impossible. Until they've genuinely risked something meaningful (their safety, a relationship, resources), they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls.

name: Salubri
bane: Third Eye. The Salubri possess a mystical third eye in the center of their forehead. The eye opens involuntarily whenever the Salubri uses a Discipline, making them obviously supernatural. The eye weeps blood when they are hungry (Hunger 4+), staining their face. The third eye cannot be surgically removed — it regenerates each night. Covering it with a bandana or hat is common but the eye sometimes opens on its own during stress.
compulsion: Affective Empathy. The vampire becomes overwhelmed by the suffering of others nearby. They must attempt to alleviate the pain, grief, or distress of the most afflicted person in the scene — through comfort, healing, or removing the source of suffering. They cannot inflict harm or ignore suffering while in this state. Until they have meaningfully eased someone's pain, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls.

name: Toreador
bane: Aesthetic Fixation. When a Toreador encounters something truly beautiful (or truly hideous) — art, a person, a sunset, an act of violence — they must roll Composure + Resolve (Difficulty = 2 + Bane Severity) or become transfixed. While fixated, they can take no actions other than contemplating the subject. The fixation lasts until the stimulus is removed, they are attacked, or the scene ends. This can trigger at catastrophically inconvenient moments.
compulsion: Obsession. The vampire becomes fixated on a specific person, object, or artistic vision in the scene. They must possess it, protect it, or perfect it — to the exclusion of all else. They will abandon other goals, ignore threats, and alienate allies in pursuit of the object of their obsession. Until they have secured their obsession or it is destroyed/removed, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls not related to the obsession.

name: Tremere
bane: Broken Blood Bond. The Tremere cannot form Blood Bonds with other Kindred — neither as regnant nor thrall. Their blood does not create loyalty in other vampires, and drinking another vampire's blood does not bind them. This makes Tremere politically unreliable — they cannot be bound to a Prince's service, and their own childer may turn against them freely. They can still Bond mortals and ghouls normally.
compulsion: Perfectionism. The vampire must meet an impossibly high standard in whatever they are currently doing. If they cannot perform a task flawlessly, they would rather not attempt it at all. They will redo completed work, refuse adequate solutions in favor of perfect ones, and criticize others' efforts. Until they achieve a critical success on a relevant roll or abandon the task entirely, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls.

name: Tzimisce
bane: Grounded. The Tzimisce must sleep in or near soil from a place meaningful to them — their birthplace, their domain, a conquered enemy's grave. Without this soil, they cannot enter restful sleep: they rise each night with a Bane Severity penalty to all dice pools until they rest properly. The soil requirement travels with them — many Tzimisce carry bags of their homeland's earth.
compulsion: Covetousness. The vampire becomes possessive of a person, object, or domain in the scene. They must assert ownership — marking territory, claiming an item, or declaring someone under their protection. They cannot share, delegate, or allow others to use the claimed thing. Until they have established undeniable possession, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls not related to the claim.

name: Ventrue
bane: Rarefied Tastes. Each Ventrue can only feed from a specific type of mortal — chosen at character creation (blue-collar workers, redheads, virgins, clergy, the frightened, criminals, CEOs, etc.). Blood from any other source tastes foul and provides no nourishment — the Ventrue's Hunger does not decrease. If desperate enough to feed on the wrong type, the Ventrue must roll Resolve + Awareness (Difficulty = 2 + Bane Severity) or vomit the blood immediately.
compulsion: Arrogance. The vampire must assert their dominance and superiority. They cannot accept help, take orders, or admit fault. They must be in charge, be right, and be acknowledged as such. Asking for assistance, apologizing, or deferring to another is impossible. Until they have forced someone to acknowledge their authority or superiority, they suffer a 2-dice penalty to all rolls.

name: Caitiff
bane: Clanless. Caitiff have no in-clan Disciplines — all Disciplines cost the out-of-clan XP rate. They are also treated with suspicion and contempt by Kindred society. Caitiff cannot claim the protection of a clan and are often the first to be sacrificed in political conflicts. On the positive side, they have no clan compulsion and can learn any Discipline without restriction.
compulsion: None. Caitiff do not have a clan compulsion. However, they lack the social support of a clan and may develop personal compulsions at the Storyteller's discretion.

name: Thin-Blood
bane: Thin Vitae. Thin-bloods are barely vampires. They can eat food, walk in dim sunlight for short periods, and may even have a heartbeat — but their blood is weak. They cannot Blood Bond other Kindred, cannot create childer reliably, and their vitae is insufficient to create ghouls. They begin with Blood Potency 0. Many Kindred consider them abominations to be destroyed on sight.
compulsion: None. Thin-bloods are too far removed from Caine to have a clan compulsion. However, their precarious existence means they face unique social and survival pressures.
